description_long: This is a pair of traditional Japanese wooden sandals known as geta, featuring thick, solid wooden soles with a natural brown grain. The sandals have a raised heel and a distinctive thong strap made of fabric, which is padded and colored in red and beige tones. The straps are attached through the front of the sole and extend to the sides, providing a secure fit for the foot. The underside of each sandal is carved to create two elevated platforms, giving the footwear its characteristic height and shape. The wood appears unfinished or only lightly treated, giving the surface a matte, slightly rough appearance. These sandals are typically worn with traditional attire but could be found in urban settings as a unique or cultural fashion statement.
description: A pair of traditional Japanese wooden geta sandals with thick brown wooden soles and red-beige fabric thong straps. The sandals feature raised heels and a natural, matte wood finish.
description_view_0: Front three-quarter view showing the thick wooden soles, red and beige fabric straps, and the raised heel structure.
description_view_1: Opposite front three-quarter view highlighting the curvature of the wooden base and the padded fabric straps.
description_view_2: Rear three-quarter view displaying the carved underside and the elevated heel platforms of the sandals.
description_view_3: Back view focusing on the heel elevation and the attachment points of the fabric straps.
